title: Restrict Dashboard view Independent of Board Access
excerpt: In our organization we have sales reps that need to be able to see a dashboard with information that they need access to like project status etc of their clients.  It would be great if we could create a custom dashboard …

posts.author: bluefoot (Jim)
posts.cookedHtml: In our organization we have sales reps that need to be able to see a dashboard with information that they need access to like project status etc of their clients. It would be great if we could create a custom dashboard that is filtered with just clients or items that they are assigned to, but only show them that dashboard when they login. Right now we have to give them access to see all the information on the board’s for any data we want to pull into a dashboard for them, which just creates confusion for the sales person plus forces us to allow each sales rep to see data for all other sales reps, unless we create a board for each sales rep along with their custom dashboard which is not what we want for our internal support team to have to know what board each client / project is on.

posts.author: Zalayeta (Mathieu)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

Any news on that?
That’s what I’m trying to achieve as well.

Cheers,

posts.author: alexm (Alex M)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bringing this to the top - we need to be able to set up dashboards to show any widgets we want, shared with anybody we want to share it with, independent of board access…

That is surely the point of a dashboard - to share selected information without needing to show the entire contents of the board.

We’re having to work around this by restricting column views for boards. For example, let’s say we have 3 columns:

  1. Revenue
  2. Costs
  3. Net Margin

If we want to share Revenue via a dashboard, we have to share the entire board and then restrict column view for ‘Costs’ and ‘Margin’.

That’s a simple example - now extrapolate that across 15-20 columns and you have a very fiddly mess.

Looking forward to a response, or if anyone knows a tidier workaround, that would also be helpful.

Best
Alex

posts.author: mmerchat (Monica Merchat)
posts.cookedHtml: I concur! It would be ideal to be able to override/set the VIEW permissions at the linked/connected column without modifying access to the entire underlying board(s)! It also makes sense for the underlying/mirrored board to accurately reflect the permissions at the linked/connected column level for transparency.

posts.author: Cherye (Cherye )
posts.cookedHtml: I am also looking for a view only solution to share dashboards. Has anyone looked into this or provided a solution.

posts.author: Atul (Atul | cloudvandana apps)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want to understand this problem statement better

So there are couple of things being talked about here

  1. you want to create a dashboard or some kind of view where you can precisely control what is gonna be shown to the users without exposing an entire dashboard and entire board to the users, right?
  2. These users can be internal or external users?
  3. You want to create a dashboard view for internal users, but they should only be able to see the items that they are “assigned to” and not any other items?

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
monday dashboards can only show data from boards a user already has access to. There’s no way today to have a single dashboard that dynamically filters itself based on the logged-in user without also giving them access to all underlying board data.

That said, here’s the cleanest pattern we’ve seen work at scale (and avoids one-board-per-rep chaos):

What works well

· Keep one master operational board (all clients/projects, all reps). This stays internal for ops/support/leadership.

· Create one shared “Sales View” board that only mirrors or connects the sales-relevant fields from the master board (status, client, key dates, etc.).

· Include a Sales Rep (People) column on that Sales View board.

· Sales reps get access only to the Sales View board — not the master board.

Dashboards

· Create one dashboard per sales rep (no board duplication).

· Each dashboard pulls from the same Sales View board and is filtered: Sales Rep → is → [that rep]

· Share only the rep’s dashboard with them.

Why this helps

· Reps only see their own clients/projects

· No confusing filters for them to manage

· Ops still works from a single source of truth

· No need to maintain a board per rep

What’s not possible (yet)

· Dashboards that auto-filter by the logged-in user

· Row-level permissions without granting board access
